originalTitle: S&P 500 ends down after Trump says Iran deal is 'over'
summary: STORY: U.S. stocks finished mostly lower on Wednesday, with the Dow dropping about 1% and the S&P 500 shedding more than a quarter of a percent. The Nasdaq, however, ticked up two-tenths of a percent. Speaking at a NATO summit, President Donald Trump said an interim deal aimed at ending the war with Iran was "over," and warned that the U.S. would likely carry out additional strikes Wednesday night. Trump's comments marked the latest setback in talks that have swung between threats of escalation and hopes for diplomacy, leaving investors wrong-footed by many false starts toward a peace deal. Chris Brigati, chief investment officer at SWBC says investors should expect more market volatility ahead. "The market got complacent. We had a lot of news coming out of the Middle East, out of Washington D.C. where there was going to be progress and peace talks, and then there was, and then there wasn't. And now it's back on again and off again. Those challenges have made it very difficult to manage these waters. And ultimately what it does is it creates volatility, and volatility creates opportunity - that's a good thing - but it's hard to be able to manage through all the volatility." :: Archive Oil prices jumped following Trump's remarks, with Brent crude futures settling up more than 5%. Among Wednesday's stock moves, SpaceX fell below its debut trading price of $150 for the second day in a row to record its lowest close since its June 12 public offering. On the flipside, shares of Broadcom rallied more than 4.5% after Apple said it plans to spend more than $30 billion as part of a chip-supply agreement reached earlier this week with the chipmaker. :: Nvidia And shares of Nvidia rose more than 3.5% after The Information reported that China plans to allow its top AI firms to buy a limited number of the company's H200 chips. Elsewhere in the market, energy price-sensitive travel stocks fell as higher oil prices stoked concerns over fuel costs and demand. United Airlines and Delta Air Lines both slipped, as did cruise operators Carnival and Norwegian Cruise Line.

chineseTitle: 特朗普表示伊朗协议“结束”后标普 500 指数收低
chineseSummary: 故事：美国股市周三收盘大多走低，道指下跌约 1%，标准普尔 500 指数跌幅超过 25%。然而，纳斯达克指数上涨了十分之二。美国总统唐纳德·特朗普在北约峰会上发表讲话时表示，旨在结束与伊朗战争的临时协议已经“结束”，并警告说美国可能会在周三晚上发动更多袭击。特朗普的言论标志着谈判的最新挫折，谈判在升级威胁和外交希望之间摇摆不定，导致投资者因和平协议的许多错误开局而措手不及。 SWBC 首席投资官克里斯·布里加蒂 (Chris Brigati) 表示，投资者应该预计未来市场会出现更多波动。 “市场变得自满。我们从中东、华盛顿特区传来很多消息，那里本来会取得进展和和平谈判，然后有，然后又没有。现在又断断续续。这些挑战使管理这些水域变得非常困难。最终，它造成了波动，而波动创造了机会——这是一件好事——但很难管理所有的波动。” :: Archive 特朗普发表讲话后，油价大幅上涨，布伦特原油期货价格上涨超过 5%。在周三的股价走势中，SpaceX 连续第二天跌破 150 美元的首次交易价格，创下自 6 月 12 日公开募股以来的最低收盘价。另一方面，在苹果表示计划斥资超过 300 亿美元作为本周早些时候与芯片制造商达成的芯片供应协议的一部分后，博通股价上涨超过 4.5%。 :: Nvidia 在 The Information 报道中国计划允许其顶级人工智能公司收购后，Nvidia 股价上涨超过 3.5%

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
